items.title: The Case of the Sun's Missing Silver
items.summary: Astronomers have solved a decades old puzzle about the Sun's missing silver, not by finding something new, but by modelling something old far more accurately. A revised, more realistic model of the…

items.title: The First Breath of Another World
items.summary: Astronomers have detected an atmosphere around a rocky planet in another star's habitable zone for the very first time. It’s a milestone that brings us a step closer to answering whether life could…

items.title: NASA's Perseverance Rover Uncovers Evidence of 4 Billion Year Old Impacts
items.summary: Old rocks exposed on the rim of Jezero Crater preserve a roughly 4-billion-year-old cosmic “weather report” from the solar system’s most dynamic era.

items.title: NASA’s Artemis III Lander Test Will be a "Dress Rehearsal" for Returning to the Moon!
items.summary: Before Artemis astronauts land on the Moon’s surface in 2028, NASA will conduct the Artemis III demonstration mission in 2027, allowing teams on Earth and in orbit to practice rendezvous and docking…

items.title: Submillimeter Array Catches a Gamma-Ray Burst Thanks to new Fast-Response System
items.summary: The Submillimeter Array's (SMA) new semi-automated alert system demonstrates how the radio interferometer quickly responds to discoveries from space-based telescopes.

items.title: The Roman Space Telescope Will Find Ancient Black Holes By Watching How They Eat Stars
items.summary: When supermassive black holes (SMBH) in a certain mass range eat a star, they first tear it apart in a Tidal Disruption Event (TDE). By detecting TDE across cosmic time, astronomers can chart the…

items.title: Satellite Images of Pengiun Poo Reveal Climate Change's Impact on the Species
items.summary: Researchers utilized 30 years of Landsat satellite imagery to analyze the color and spectral signatures of Adélie penguin guano across Antarctica, marking the first time space-based observations have…

items.title: This Exoplanet Hid for 10 Years Before Astronomers Finally Found It
items.summary: A team of astronomers have discovered a third planet orbiting the star Beta Pictoris. The new planet, Beta Pictoris d, is 100 times fainter than Beta Pictoris b — the first planet discovered in the…

items.title: After a Billion Kilometres, China's Asteroid Hunter Finally Arrives
items.summary: After chasing a small asteroid across a billion kilometres of space, China's Tianwen-2 probe has finally caught up, closing to within twenty kilometres of its target and beginning detailed scientific…

items.title: A Relativistic Jet Could be an Indication of the 'Missing-Link' for Black Holes
